title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Research
My research interests center around the intersection between sport and the environment, specifically on the ability of sport to encourage pro-environmental behavior and action. I focus on identity-based theoretical approaches and message framing. Methodologically, I utilize primarily quantitative methods within a pragmatic paradigm, with a focus on Bayesian statistical approaches.

Peer-Reviewed Journal Articles
Warwick, L., Oliveira, J. R., Sato, M. (2025). Investigating the Climate Vulnerability of North American Ultra-Endurance Running Events. Sport in Society. https://doi.org/10.1080/17430437.2025.2450797
Sato, M., Warwick, L., Pitas, N., Liechty, T. (2025). Park and Recreational Facility Availability, Leisure-Time Physical Activity, Socioeconomic Status, and Race. Leisure Sciences, 1–24. https://doi.org/10.1080/01490400.2025.2566939
Sato, M., Tokuyama, T., Motoyama, S., Warwick, L., Deguchi, J. (2024). Examining switching intention in professional sport: a push-pull-mooring perspective. International Journal of Sports Marketing and Sponsorship, 25(4), 895–912. https://doi.org/10.1108/IJSMS-12-2023-0245
Foti, L., Warwick, L., Lyons, E., Dhaliwal, S., Alcorn, M. (2023). Knowledge Transfer and Innovation: Universities as Catalysts for Sustainable Decision Making in Industry. Sustainability, 15(14), 11175. https://doi.org/10.3390/su151411175
O’Reilly, N., Hernandez, T., Séguin, B., Warwick, L., & Hasani, T. (2023). Barriers to youth sport participation in dense urban centres. International Journal of Sport Management and Marketing, 23(3), 175. https://doi.org/10.1504/IJSMM.2023.130696
O’Reilly, N., Nadeau, J., & Warwick, L. (2022). Adventure tourism and the place brand of Argentina. Journal of Sport & Tourism, 26(3), 297–313. https://doi.org/10.1080/14775085.2022.2094991

title: Welcome To My Blog
text:
This is the first post in a Quarto blog. Welcome!

Since this post doesn’t specify an explicit image, the first image in the post will be used in the listing page of posts.

title: Hello, Hej, Bonjour!
text:
Work
I’m an assistant professor in the Marketing and Sport Management department at Ithaca College.
I currently teach Sport Marketing, Sport Sales & Sponsorship, and Sustainability in Sport.
My research focuses on the intersection of sport and sustainability, with a particular emphasis on consumer behavior and marketing strategies that promote sustainable practices in the sport industry. I’m also interested in open science, questions of causality, and the use of bayesian statistics in sport management research.
Outside of the ivory tower, I enjoy cycling, skiing, and spending time with my wife and our two cats, Reggie & Rosie. I’m a fan of the Toronto Maple Leafs, Toronto Sceptres, Toronto Blue Jays, Arsenal FC, and have recently got into cricket. Current Fan Misery Index: 70
